summary: Stancil argues that while LLM demos look impressive, they fail in real-world data environments because messy schemas, ambiguous definitions, and complex queries overwhelm current models. The real revolution will come not from smarter models alone but from rebuilding data structures to be AI-friendly—standardized, machine-readable schemas that shift analytics work from bespoke design to factory-like production.
money_quotes: The distance between this and companies’ actual data ecosystems is staggering.
The progress bar is a mirage.
Rather than asking self-driving cars to navigate hopelessly complex terrain, we could bulldoze the terrain into something more manageable.
What if instead of building new patterns for people, we built them for our AI gods?
If that changes, if we design our worlds for AIs, if we give our power and strength unto the beast, we might be able to go much further than we can today.
key_insight: AI won’t transform analytics by replacing analysts outright; it will force a redesign of data schemas themselves so machines—not people—can reliably interpret and query them.
